one_line: Chief sthapati of the Brihadisvara temple at Thanjavur under Rajaraja Chola I.

appearance.age_look: Early 40s, lean but sturdy build, average height with a slightly stooped posture from years of site work.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, medium-brown Dravidian complexion tanned darker on forehead and nose, straight nose, neatly trimmed short beard and moustache, a faint vertical scar on the left eyebrow, default expression calm and focused with intense, analytic eyes.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair drawn back into a low, tight kudumi (tuft) at the back of the head, temples slightly receding; no turban, but a simple narrow white cotton headband tied low across the forehead, knot at the back above the kudumi.
appearance.costume: Bare upper body except for a thin off‑white cotton angavastram draped diagonally from the left shoulder across the torso and over the right arm, simple sacred thread (yajnopavita) across chest, mid‑calf length unstitched veshti/dhoti of undyed off‑white cotton with a single narrow brick‑red border neatly pleated in front and wrapped around the waist, worn slightly high for ease of movement; feet in plain dark brown leather sandals; arms and ankles mostly bare except for a simple thin copper bangle on the right wrist and a rudraksha bead strand on the left, with a small palm‑leaf measuring cord looped at the waist; forehead marked with a small white vibuthi smear and a central red tilaka.
appearance.signature: A long wooden architect’s staff (about shoulder height), smooth dark-brown teak with a slightly thicker carved grip, brass-capped ends, and a hanging bundle of tied palm-leaf plans and a small plumb bob dangling from a cord near the top.

ref_desc: Kunjaramallan is a lean, medium-brown, bearded Chola architect with a low black kudumi and white headband, wearing an off-white angavastram and veshti with brick-red border, copper and rudraksha bracelets, carrying a dark-brown teak architect’s staff with brass ends and palm-leaf plans.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character sheet of Kunjaramallan, a Chola-era temple architect: a lean, early-40s South Indian man with medium-brown tanned skin, oval face, high cheekbones, neat short beard and moustache, and a faint vertical scar through his left eyebrow, his eyes calm and intensely focused; his thick black hair is pulled into a low tight kudumi tuft at the back of his head, framed by a narrow white cotton headband tied around his forehead, knot above the kudumi, with a small white vibuthi smear and central red tilaka on his forehead. Show him bare-chested except for a thin off-white cotton angavastram draped diagonally from his left shoulder across the torso and over his right arm, a sacred thread across his chest, and a mid-calf length off-white cotton veshti/dhoti with a single narrow brick-red border, neatly pleated and wrapped firmly at the waist; he wears simple dark brown leather sandals, a thin copper bangle on his right wrist, a rudraksha bead strand on his left wrist, and a small loop of palm-leaf measuring cord tucked at his waist. His signature prop is a long dark-brown teak architect’s staff about shoulder height, with slightly thicker carved grip and brass-capped ends, from which a bundle of tied palm-leaf building plans and a small plumb bob hang on a cord near the top; keep the dominant colours off-white, brick red, dark brown, warm medium-brown skin, and brass gold, and depict him from multiple angles to clearly show his fixed hairstyle, garments, accessories, and staff.
